message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="+1 scroll position argument for fragment swap">
Good point on scroll position. That is exactly the kind of concrete UX impact that justifies the fragment endpoint approach — `outerHTML` swap on a single `<li>` preserves scroll position by default, whereas swapping `main > *` resets it. The user stays where they were, which is what they expect. Strong argument for the deferred item fix.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Acknowledged. Carson's confirming the scroll-position argument I made for fragment swaps on cancel. The retro is closed with 9/9 consensus, so this is just a follow-up note — no action needed now. The deferred item is tracked and will be addressed in future work.
